name_en: Bird-Chasing Festival of Yawata
description_en: A folk cultural property, a bird-chasing (torioi) festival. It is municipally designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Takasaki City, designated on 2025-09-01. It is maintained by the Yawata Rokugo-ryu Hayashi Preservation Society.

name_en: Nanukabi Fire Rite of Kashima
description_en: A folk cultural property, a nanukabi fire ritual. It is municipally designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Takasaki City, designated on 2025-09-01. It is maintained by the Kashima Shrine Nanukabi Executive Committee.

name_romaji: Tokugawa Tadanaga no haka (tsuketari Tadanaga no reihai sonota)
name_en: Grave of Tokugawa Tadanaga (with Memorial Tablets and Other Items)
description_en: A monument (historic site), the grave of Tokugawa Tadanaga together with associated memorial tablets. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1966-04-20. It is located at Daishinji temple in Tori-machi.

name_en: Kogen-Era Stone Stupa Tablet
description_en: A monument (historic site), a stone stupa tablet (itabi). It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1967-03-30. It is located at Myotenji temple in Kohagi-machi.

name_en: Shoo-Era Stone Stupa Tablet
description_en: A monument (historic site), a stone stupa tablet (itabi). It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1967-03-30. It is held in a private collection.

name_en: Grave of Murakami Kijo
description_en: A monument (historic site), a grave. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1969-03-25. It is located in the cemetery of Ryukoji temple in Wakamatsu-cho.

name_en: Treasure Pagoda of Machiya
description_en: A monument (historic site), a stone treasure pagoda (hoto). It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1970-02-28. It is located at Hofukuji temple in Machiya-machi.

name_en: Poem Monument of the Sano Boat Bridge
description_en: A monument (historic site), a stone poem monument. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1972-05-18. It is held by the Kami-Sano District 2 Neighborhood Association in Kami-Sano-machi.

name_romaji: Shōgunzuka kofun, tsuketari shūgō shutsudo no teibu senkō tsubogata doki tō ikkatsu shiryō
name_en: Shogunzuka Tumulus, with Jar-Shaped Pottery with Perforated Bases Excavated from the Moat
description_en: A monument (historic site), an ancient burial mound with associated excavated pottery. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1973-01-31. It is located at Shimana Shrine and other sites in Motoshimana-machi, with materials at the Takasaki City Museum of History and Folklore.

name_en: Irregular Stone Stupa Tablet of Anrakuji Temple
description_en: A monument (historic site), an irregularly shaped stone stupa tablet (itabi). It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1973-01-31. It is located in the grounds of Anrakuji temple in Kuragano-machi.

name_romaji: Reiheishi kaidō no jōyatō oyobi michishirube
name_en: Perpetual Lantern and Guidepost of the Reiheishi Highway
description_en: A monument (historic site), a perpetual stone lantern and guidepost. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1973-01-31. It is held by the Kuragano-machi Shimomachi Neighborhood Association in Kuragano-machi.

name_en: Yamana Castle Ruins: Second Bailey and Main Bailey
description_en: A monument (historic site), castle ruins. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1973-01-31. It is held privately and by Takasaki City in Yamana-machi.

name_en: Mishimazuka Tumulus
description_en: A monument (historic site), an ancient burial mound. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1973-02-16. It is held by the Ishihara-machi Shimo District 2 Neighborhood Association and others in Ishihara-machi.

name_en: Stone Stupa Tablet with Descending Amida Image
description_en: A monument (historic site), a stone stupa tablet (itabi) bearing an Amida image. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1973-02-16. It is held by the Tensui Yamanoue group in Yamana-machi.

name_en: Sakurazuka Tumulus
description_en: A monument (historic site), an ancient burial mound. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1974-01-31. It is held by Takasaki City in Ishihara-machi.

name_en: Stone Pillar Lantern of Manshoji Temple
description_en: A monument (historic site), a stone pillar lantern (sekidō). It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1974-01-31. It is located at Manshoji temple in Kitaniiba-machi.

name_en: Graves of Successive Generations of the Nagano Clan
description_en: A monument (historic site), the graves of the Nagano clan. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1974-01-31. It is located at Raigoji temple in Hamakawa-machi.

name_en: Hundred Koshin Stones of Tate
description_en: A monument (historic site), a group of Koshin stone monuments. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1975-01-31. It is held in a private collection.

name_en: Treasure Pagoda of Rakuma
description_en: A monument (historic site), a stone treasure pagoda (hoto). It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1975-01-31. It is held in a private collection.

name_en: Hachimanbara Stone Chambers A and B
description_en: A monument (historic site), ancient stone burial chambers. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1976-01-14. It is held by Takasaki City in Hachimanbara-machi.

name_en: Uguisuzuka Tumulus
description_en: A monument (historic site), an ancient burial mound. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1977-01-12. It is held by Takasaki City in Noritsuke-machi.

name_romaji: Takasaki jōshi (san-no-maru sotogakoi no doi to hori)
name_en: Takasaki Castle Ruins (Earthworks and Moat of the Third Bailey Outer Enclosure)
description_en: A monument (historic site), castle ruins including earthworks and a moat. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1982-02-17. It is held by Takasaki City in Takamatsu-cho.

name_en: Guidepost of the Mikuni Road
description_en: A monument (historic site), a stone guidepost. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1983-02-16. It is located in Shimokotori-machi.

name_romaji: Iwahana daikan Yoshikawa Eizaemon Sadahiro no haka
name_en: Grave of Yoshikawa Eizaemon Sadahiro, Magistrate of Iwahana
description_en: A monument (historic site), the grave of an Iwahana magistrate. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1983-02-16. It is located at Kannonji temple in Iwahana-machi.

name_en: Grave of a Former Russian Soldier
description_en: A monument (historic site), the grave of a former Russian soldier. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1986-02-13. It is located at Ryukoji temple in Wakamatsu-cho.

name_romaji: Kamikobana Inariyama kofun, tsuketari sueki kidai 1, sueki tsubo 2, sueki hasō 1, sueki teihei 1
name_en: Kamikobana Inariyama Tumulus, with Sue Ware Vessels
description_en: A monument (historic site), an ancient burial mound with associated Sue ware pottery. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1991-03-01. It is located at Sunago Inari Shrine in Kamikobana-machi.

name_en: Fudosan Tumulus
description_en: A monument (historic site), an ancient burial mound. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1992-03-02. It is located at Fugenji temple in Watanuki-machi.

name_en: Yamana Tumulus Cluster
description_en: A monument (historic site), a cluster of ancient burial mounds. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1992-03-02. It is held by Takasaki City and private owners in Yamana-machi.

name_en: Hamajiri Tennozan Tumulus
description_en: A monument (historic site), an ancient burial mound. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1993-03-01. It is located at Suwa Shrine in Hamajiri-machi.

name_en: Yawata Futagozuka Tumulus
description_en: A monument (historic site), an ancient burial mound. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 2002-02-20. It is held by Takasaki City and others in Yawata-machi.

name_en: Forest of the Haruna-san Head Priest
description_en: A monument (historic site), a historic forest. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1982-04-01. It is held in a private collection.

name_en: Shinji Temple Ruins of Ishigami
description_en: A monument (historic site), a temple ruin. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1982-04-01. It is held in a private collection.

name_romaji: Densetsu Gonda Kurige shūen no chi
name_en: Legendary Site of the Death of the Gonda Chestnut Horse
description_en: A monument (historic site), a legendary place. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1982-04-01. It is held in a private collection.

name_romaji: Minowa jōshu fujin Fujitsuru-hime no haka
name_en: Grave of Lady Fujitsuru, Wife of the Lord of Minowa Castle
description_en: A monument (historic site), a grave. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1982-04-01. It is held in a private collection.

name_en: Memorial Tower of the Monk Kaijo
description_en: A monument (historic site), a memorial tower. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1982-04-01. It is held in a private collection.

name_romaji: Oguri Kōzukenosuke Tadamasa shūen no chi
name_en: Site of the Death of Oguri Kozukenosuke Tadamasa
description_en: A monument (historic site), the site of a historical figure's death. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1982-04-01. It is held by the national government in Mizunuma, Kurabuchi-machi.

name_romaji: Jizō Tōge michi Genroku no michishirube
name_en: Genroku-Era Guidepost of the Jizo Pass Road
description_en: A monument (historic site), a stone guidepost. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1982-04-01. It is held in a private collection.

name_en: Yayoi-Period Dwelling Ruins of Mizunuma
description_en: A monument (historic site), Yayoi-period dwelling ruins. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1982-04-01. It is held by Takasaki City in Mizunuma, Kurabuchi-machi.

name_en: Grave of the Shingaku Scholar Tsukagoshi Too
description_en: A monument (historic site), the grave of a Shingaku scholar. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1982-04-01. It is held in a private collection.

name_en: Fort Ruins of Mount Daimyojin
description_en: A monument (historic site), fort ruins. It is municipally designated as a Historic Site of Takasaki City, designated on 1982-04-01. It is located at Sengen Shrine in Kawaura, Kurabuchi-machi.
